channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: Palm Sunday
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: Palm Sunday

item.title: Palm Sunday, These Things They Did Not Understand At First, 2008-04-21
item.description: :Palm Sunday. These Things They Did Not Understand At First, 2008-04-21
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. These Things They Did Not Understand At First, 2008-04-21

item.title: Palm Sunday, The inner meaning of Palm Sunday., 2009-04-12
item.description: :Palm Sunday. The events of Palm Sunday are momentous, but they cannot be understood without understanding the words of the Apostle Paul and contrasting them to the actions of the people who received Jesus joyfully and with shouts of praise as he rode into Jerusalem on a colt, the foal of an ass.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. The events of Palm Sunday are momentous, but they cannot be understood without understanding the words of the Apostle Paul and contrasting them to the actions of the people who received Jesus joyfully and with shouts of praise as he rode into Jerusalem on a colt, the foal of an ass.

item.title: Palm Sunday, Palm Sunday, Why did we read this Epistle? Principles of scriptural exegesis. , 2010-03-28
item.description: :Palm Sunday. Palm Sunday, Why did we read this Epistle? Principles of scriptural exegesis. , 2010-03-28
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. Palm Sunday, Why did we read this Epistle? Principles of scriptural exegesis. , 2010-03-28

item.title: Palm Sunday, Learning Humility By Recounting The Events Of Holy Week, 2011-04-17
item.description: :Palm Sunday. A very short homily after Vespers in the vigil for Palm Sunday. The events of Holy week should teach us humility. Many mistakes were made, by those who loved the Lord and those who hated Him. For instance, two prophesies, one from Zechariah, and the other from Jeremiah, were fulfilled to the letter, and the leaders who plotted to destroy Jesus, and who knew the Scripture, were blind to this. Martha and Mary, who loved the Lord, showed deep ingnorance about Who He is. We must learn humility from these examples.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. A very short homily after Vespers in the vigil for Palm Sunday. The events of Holy week should teach us humility. Many mistakes were made, by those who loved the Lord and those who hated Him. For instance, two prophesies, one from Zechariah, and the other from Jeremiah, were fulfilled to the letter, and the leaders who plotted to destroy Jesus, and who knew the Scripture, were blind to this. Martha and Mary, who loved the Lord, showed deep ingnorance about Who He is. We must learn humility from these examples.

item.title: Palm Sunday, Rejoice In The Lord Alway And Again I Say Rejoice, 2011-04-17
item.description: :Palm Sunday. A homily by Deacon Nicholas Park in which he discusses the admonition of the Apostle Paul to "Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice". Many if the children of Jerusalem who cried "Hosanna" were worshipping Jesus as the person they *wanted* Him to be, and not as He really is.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. A homily by Deacon Nicholas Park in which he discusses the admonition of the Apostle Paul to "Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice". Many if the children of Jerusalem who cried "Hosanna" were worshipping Jesus as the person they *wanted* Him to be, and not as He really is.

item.title: Palm Sunday, 2012-04-08
item.description: :Palm Sunday. Why do we read this epistle on Palm Sunday, since it is not about suffering, or the cross or the resurrection? There are many other epistles about these things, but this epistle contains instructions that must be applied during Holy Week in order to truly benefit from Pascha. We can understand how to apply the Apostle Paul's instructions by looking at the good and bad examples in today's Gospel, such as Mary, who annointed the Lord, Judas, the people, and the high priests. There are many lessons here, and all of Holy Week will contain many more. The Lord is at hand!
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. Why do we read this epistle on Palm Sunday, since it is not about suffering, or the cross or the resurrection? There are many other epistles about these things, but this epistle contains instructions that must be applied during Holy Week in order to truly benefit from Pascha. We can understand how to apply the Apostle Paul's instructions by looking at the good and bad examples in today's Gospel, such as Mary, who annointed the Lord, Judas, the people, and the high priests. There are many lessons here, and all of Holy Week will contain many more. The Lord is at hand!

item.title: Palm Sunday, Palm Sunday, 2013-04-28
item.description: :Palm Sunday. The Entry into Jerusalem is not only a historical event, but also an allegory of how God enters the soul and either abides in it or is cast out. We look at all the symbolic meanings of Jerusalem, the children, the rulers, the prophesy of the destruction of the temple, etc. What will we do so that when Jesus enters our Jerusalem, he stays?.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. The Entry into Jerusalem is not only a historical event, but also an allegory of how God enters the soul and either abides in it or is cast out. We look at all the symbolic meanings of Jerusalem, the children, the rulers, the prophesy of the destruction of the temple, etc. What will we do so that when Jesus enters our Jerusalem, he stays?.

item.title: Palm Sunday, Palm Sunday, 2013-04-28
item.description: :Palm Sunday. During the entry into Jerusalem, there was a marked contrast: the children praised Jesus and proclaimed Him as God with great joy, but the leaders were "sore displeased". Why were they so indignant? It was because of their sins and passions, especially their anger and pride. They teach us how to be like children and have joy, if we will listen. It is clear that any unrepented of sin makes us like the leaders, incapable of accepting the Lord into our Jerusalem - our soul, and feeling joy. Let us learn how to be like children by observing their behavior
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. During the entry into Jerusalem, there was a marked contrast: the children praised Jesus and proclaimed Him as God with great joy, but the leaders were "sore displeased". Why were they so indignant? It was because of their sins and passions, especially their anger and pride. They teach us how to be like children and have joy, if we will listen. It is clear that any unrepented of sin makes us like the leaders, incapable of accepting the Lord into our Jerusalem - our soul, and feeling joy. Let us learn how to be like children by observing their behavior

item.title: Palm Sunday, 2014-04-13
item.description: :Palm Sunday. Today is basically from my heart to yours, with a bit of incoherence and great love. The epistle today tells us the Lord is at hand, and we are standing with palms and pussy willows in our hands, emulating the children of Israel. A large portion of those who held palms when the Lord was at hand in the entry into Jerusalem later were complicit in His crucifixion, and a large portion of Orthodox who have not been in church for a great while hold palms on a great church holiday. How are we going to be different? The apostle also tells us to rejoice always - how doe this apply to the raising of Lazarus, which moist of you never hear in church, and the things in your life that are four days dead?
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Palm Sunday. Today is basically from my heart to yours, with a bit of incoherence and great love. The epistle today tells us the Lord is at hand, and we are standing with palms and pussy willows in our hands, emulating the children of Israel. A large portion of those who held palms when the Lord was at hand in the entry into Jerusalem later were complicit in His crucifixion, and a large portion of Orthodox who have not been in church for a great while hold palms on a great church holiday. How are we going to be different? The apostle also tells us to rejoice always - how doe this apply to the raising of Lazarus, which moist of you never hear in church, and the things in your life that are four days dead?
